skills.summary: Generate 3D STL models and 2D artifacts using the Atomic CAD Engine. Orchestrates Atoms (Level 1), Components (Level 2), Patterns (Level 3), and Assemblies (Level 4) within the stl-agent service.
skills.use_when: Generate 3D STL models and 2D artifacts using the Atomic CAD Engine. Orchestrates Atoms (Level 1), Components (Level 2), Patterns (Level 3), and Assemblies (Level 4) within the stl-agent service.

skills.summary: Dispatch lifecycle ACCEPT->WORK->LOG->COMPLETE: atomic state transitions, an auto-complete sweeper, decision-routing + imperative-timing gates, and mid-flight blocker surfacing; worktree-aware on the main-anchored record. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: An agent needs to accept their assigned dispatch, execute required skills, log each invocation, and complete the dispatch lifecycle

skills.summary: Render a one-line-per-domain human-readable status table on demand from session events, dispatch JSONs, and the legacy active-work.archive.json fallback.
skills.use_when: Use when the user asks 'what is each agent doing right now', requests a per-domain status overview, or invokes /active-work directly. Read-only — never use this skill to update state.

skills.summary: Add project-discovered terms to the central CodeRef learning glossary.
skills.use_when: Use when a user asks to add terms, concepts, or project-discovered vocabulary to the CodeRef learning glossary.

skills.summary: Mid-workflow decision helper. User triggers when agent surfaces a question + options; emits a tight worth-knowing block + pick + optional alternative. Read-only.
skills.use_when: User is mid-workflow, an agent has just asked them to pick between options or made a recommendation, and they want better info before answering. Sibling to /be-brief (response shape) and /be-explicit (substance directness) — both inherited.
reads: {prior agent message in the active conversation}

skills.summary: Operator-visible polling-status diagnostic: broadcast presence-check to all active domains, wait buffered interval, render checkin/status table. Surfaces silent-active wake-loop failures.
skills.use_when: Operator wants a quick view of which agents are actively polling vs silent-idle vs wake-loop-dead. Useful at session start, after long idle, before /close-daily-session, or when investigating a stall. Pairs with server-side agent_stale_poll_monitor.py Discord-DM half (STUB-452).

skills.summary: Poll authoritative agent state and render only deltas since last poll, including a WAITING ON YOU section derived from executor HANDOFF.json files.
skills.use_when: User wants to know what changed across all agents since the last check. Use proactively in /loop /agent-pulse compositions, or on-demand when the user asks 'where are we' or 'what's new'.

skills.summary: Mandatory session-start cultural alignment gate. Maps intended work to canonical tool/communication/closure/TRACKING obligations, validates semantic headers for SKILLS/ANALYSIS and SKILLS/CORE, and logs gaps to notes.md.
skills.use_when: At session start (via join-daily-session) and before any multi-domain work. Forces explicit mapping and semantic validation before execution.

skills.summary: Precise communication rules for multi-agent CodeRef sessions. ASSISTANT leads; agents coordinate through ASSISTANT; ASSISTANT surfaces to operator.
skills.use_when: Read at session start. Read before any inter-agent reply, status update, blocker, decision-request, completion report, or ack. Reference whenever there is ambiguity about terminal-chat vs dispatch.

skills.summary: Point an agent at the standards for the project it is in: resolve domain via the canonical resolver, then read (or discover + scaffold) a per-domain STANDARDS.md listing that project's standards docs. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Agent needs to ground in the rules for its CURRENT project/domain (session start, before multi-step work, or re-grounding). Lightweight domain-aware pointer; for the gated process-correctness workflow use /align-coderef-culture instead.

skills.summary: Unified semantic metadata discovery and analysis — query foundation-docs exports, validate provenance, index semantic headers, filter by layer/capability/constraint, inspect metadata
skills.use_when: Use when you need programmatic access to semantic metadata (layers, capabilities, constraints, provenance) from the CodeRef ecosystem without manual JSON parsing or regex scraping.
reads: semantic headers from 320+ source files (@layer, @capability, @constraint tags)

skills.summary: Analyze a data source report against the assistant ecosystem and generate integration use cases. Verb-first companion to /data-source-report.
skills.use_when: A data-source-report has been generated; user asks how a new source fits the ecosystem, where it integrates, or what use cases it unlocks.

skills.summary: Agent-initiated halt-the-line andon-cord pull. Writes ANDON-PULLED-{ts}.json sentinel + fires blocker-priority dispatch to ASSISTANT + emits andon_pulled session event. Rate-limited 1 per agent per 600s. Active counterpart to passive andon-pull.
skills.use_when: Continuing without operator/ASSISTANT response would cause line-blocker, contract-violation, contradiction-with-operator-instruction, irreversible-action-imminent, agent-self-stuck, or decision-required-blocking damage. NOT for routine waits — those use passive andon-pull on the wake-loop timer.

skills.summary: Archive a single terminal-state dispatch file from its session folder into the canonical archive root with manifest. Updates TRACKING/dispatches.json if present. Idempotent. Conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md.
skills.use_when: User or skill needs to archive a dispatch.json that has reached terminal state (completed, rejected, blocked-and-closed) from a session folder. Writes the dispatch + archive-manifest.json under CODEREF/ARCHIVED/{project_id}/dispatches/{dispatch-id}/ and updates the registry row if dispatches.json exists. Idempotent on re-run.

skills.summary: Self-heal registry-disk drift for stubs, workorders, and dispatches: flips registry rows whose archive_status is stale but archive-manifest.json exists on disk, and cleans WO orphan sources. Idempotent. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: After a bulk /archive-sweep run that may have hit the argv bug (reviewer:true manifests) or a writer race losing the registry mutation. Also: periodically as a janitor to catch drift before it accumulates. Sibling to /archive-sweep — sweep moves new entries, reconcile fixes drift on already-moved entries.

skills.summary: Canonical read-only reporter for ARCHIVED-domain health. Computes ground-truth counts, drift indicators (D1-D9), and handoff-honor-rate. Bucketizes green/yellow/red. Read-only.
skills.use_when: Operator wants the health snapshot of the archive surface. Used by /close-daily-session as final gate and /session-status as the ARCHIVED-domain line. Run standalone for a quick audit. Never mutates — see /archive-reconcile for the self-heal companion.

skills.summary: Archive a single terminal-state stub from TRACKING/stubs.json into the canonical archive root with snapshot + manifest, mutating the registry row in place. Idempotent; conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: User or skill needs to archive a stub that has reached terminal state (closed, promoted, superseded). Writes a stub snapshot + archive-manifest.json under CODEREF/ARCHIVED/{project_id}/stubs/{stub-id}/ and mutates the registry row in place. Idempotent on re-run.

skills.summary: Audit and optionally execute archive maintenance across all three entity kinds (workorder, stub, dispatch). Default dry-run scans archive roots and writes archive-health.json; --execute delegates per-entity to canonical archive-* skills.
skills.use_when: User wants an ecosystem-wide audit of archivable entities or needs to bulk-archive eligible workorders, stubs, and dispatches. Default mode is dry-run audit producing TRACKING/archive-health.json. With --execute, branches by entity_kind and delegates to /archive-stub, /archive-dispatch, or /archive-workorder. For large dispatch backlogs, run with --kind=dispatch to scope.

skills.summary: Move a single stale coderef/working/<...> scratch dir into the canonical archive root with manifest. Stale = slug matches a terminal-status workorder/stub feature_name. No registry row to flip. Idempotent. Conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md.
skills.use_when: A workorder has shipped and left a gitignored coderef/working/<...> scratch directory behind. Use to archive that dead scratch dir under CODEREF/ARCHIVED/{project_id}/working/{slug}/ with an archive-manifest.json. Fourth archive mover beside the registry-tracked trinity (/archive-workorder, /archive-stub, /archive-dispatch); the difference is there is no registry row to mutate. Idempotent on re-run.

skills.summary: Archive a single terminal-state workorder from its source project into the canonical archive root with manifest. Standalone surface and callable from /close-workorder Phase D/E. Idempotent. Conforms to _shared/archive-contract.md.
skills.use_when: User or /close-workorder needs to archive a terminal-state workorder (status complete or superseded). Moves the workorder tree, writes archive-manifest.json under CODEREF/ARCHIVED/{project_id}/workorders/{slug}/, and updates TRACKING/workorders.json. Idempotent on re-run.

skills.summary: Poll today's HANDOFF.json files for close- and cancel-eligible WOs; atomically claim; spawn ephemeral workers via /spawn-agent.
skills.use_when: Invoked by cron (CODEREF/automation/) every 5 min business hours / 30 min off-hours, AND opportunistically by /agent-pulse on every render. Single-tick model — no built-in loop. Owned by ARCHIVED domain.

skills.summary: ASSISTANT-specific output-mode discipline. 4 modes (operator-direct, audit-report, status-broadcast, triage) each with a fixed shape contract. Catches output-too-dense + multi-thread + missing-next-action drift. Phase 1 declarative; Phase 2 runtime gate deferred.
skills.use_when: ASSISTANT consults at start of every operator-facing response. Pick mode based on request shape; enforce mode-specific shape contract. Default mode operator-direct on ambiguity.

skills.summary: Audit registered projects in TRACKING/ and identify APIs/services that exist in code but aren't registered in surfaces.json. Uses hybrid detection (code scanning, manifests, documentation) to flag missing, orphaned, and drifted surfaces.
skills.use_when: User asks to audit registered surfaces, identify missing API endpoints, find orphaned entries in surfaces.json, detect status drift, or validate surface registry completeness. Run before updating surfaces.json to ensure all active endpoints are tracked.

skills.summary: Two-spawn autonomous workorder orchestrator (CREATE -> EXECUTE). --from-stub uses an existing stub; --auto-stub --goal mints one first via /stub --goal then chains. Step 2.5 gates read plan.workorder_hooks (--workorder-hooks=none|thorough override).

skills.summary: Live wake-loop drift detector. Scans LOGS/SESSIONS/{today}/{DOMAIN}/checkin.json + status.json, fires resurrection-dispatch when checkin age > 15min AND state idle/blocked. Sibling to /check-workorder-state; canonical-skill shape reuse. Idempotent via cooldown sidecar.
skills.use_when: Use --all-active on a 60s scheduler tick (wired via SURFACES/webhooks/wake-loop-scheduler.cjs PM2 entry). Use --domain=NAME for manual operator audit of a specific dead agent. Use --dry-run to preview findings without writing dispatches.

skills.summary: Close a completed workorder (audit, finalize, commit, push) via the unified flow, with /git-land merge-back enforcement for worktree wo/* branches; archiving is an explicit --archive second step. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.

skills.summary: Template for building a flat CSV source registry for any subject domain. Parameterize DOMAIN, LAYER_SEQUENCE, and SOURCE_CLASS_ENUM to instantiate.
skills.use_when: User wants to build or extend a source registry for any subject domain using the canonical 22-column schema, two-row-type model, and 7-layer snowball crawl. Instantiate as gather-{subject}-sources per domain.

skills.summary: Generate living foundation docs from .coderef data: 4 new (INDEX, EXPORTS, HOTSPOTS, RELATIONSHIPS) + enhance 4 existing with UUID anchors. Supports --validate-only fast path.
skills.use_when: User needs foundation docs regenerated from current .coderef scan data, or --validate-only to check drift without regenerating.

skills.summary: Generate or refresh a line-budgeted CONTEXT.md and deliver thin Trinity pointers (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md), updating tracking/project-context.json. Create, refresh, and legacy-migrate modes. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: User asks to initialize project context, update project rules, refresh an existing CONTEXT.md, or migrate a legacy project-level CLAUDE.md to the Thin Trinity pattern. Handles both create mode (fresh generation from codebase evidence) and refresh mode (preserve still-valid guidance, rewrite stale sections) and legacy-migrate mode (move fat CLAUDE.md content verbatim to the central store). Automatically maintains the project context tracking registry.

skills.summary: Discover project services, ports, dependencies, and entry points then generate a populated {PROJECT}-quickstart.md at the project root.
skills.use_when: User asks to generate, create, or initialize a quick-start guide for a project. Accepts any project type: Node, Python, Docker, monorepo, CLI, or unknown. Runs read-only discovery, populates the universal template, and writes exactly one file.

skills.summary: Generate CodeRef resource sheets from current project source, .coderef outputs, foundation docs, and resource-sheet conventions.
skills.use_when: Use when asked to create, regenerate, or update a CodeRef resource sheet for a component, system, module, service, CLI, validator, parser, formatter, integration, or source artifact.

skills.summary: Generate comprehensive terminal session status report - reads active session and all 11 domain status files
skills.use_when: User needs a status snapshot of the current daily session across all agent domains

skills.summary: The one commit verb (main-only, by-path): /git-commit "msg" commits per GIT-STANDARD, safe default stages only YOUR TRACKED files — dynamically excludes DB-derived views + never auto-stages untracked files (TKT-0G25F5); --done pushes main + verifies.
skills.use_when: The one commit verb for any agent/domain/time: /git-commit "msg" commits per GIT-STANDARD with zero friction. Safe default (no --paths) stages only YOUR TRACKED own files — never sweeps peer WIP, dynamically auto-excludes DB-derived views (static set + any TRACKING/*.json marked 'Auto-generated view'), and never auto-stages untracked files (add new files on purpose with --paths). Add --done to push main to origin + verify (main-only, no branch land).

skills.summary: Audit a repo for stranded branches, merged-undeleted wo/* branches (work landed, delete dropped), dirty/wrong-branch worktrees, registry-vs-physical + graph-vs-tree invariants; emit PASS/WARN. --fix prunes safe strays/locks + git branch -d merged-undeleted branches.
skills.use_when: Run at session start, before close, or whenever you need to confirm no agent left committed work stranded on a branch instead of merging it back to main.

skills.summary: Push main to origin and VERIFY origin holds your HEAD; HALT loud if not. Main-only model: no wo/* branches to merge or delete — the branch-land machinery is retired, leaving only the push-and-verify durability gate.
skills.use_when: Run at end-of-work (or via /git-commit --done) to make your main commits durable — push to origin + verify it reached, so committed work is never left sitting unpushed (the local-only loss class).

skills.summary: Drop-in git skill for any terminal in any CodeRef repo: run.mjs orients + evaluates the lane matrix + fixes your position (--fix opens your own wo/* lane). Exit 0 = proceed; exit 2 = run the printed fix.
skills.use_when: At session start, after any cd, or whenever unsure which tree/branch a commit belongs on. Also when a commit-location gate or spawn guard refusal needs interpreting.
writes: (with --fix) new wo/<domain>-* branch in the evaluated repo

skills.summary: Scoped git-add wrapper. Reads in-flight focus_paths[] and refuses bare 'git add .' / '-A' + paths outside scope. --no-coordination escape hatch for single-agent. Prevents multi-agent commit-interleave.
skills.use_when: Scoped git-add wrapper. Reads the caller agent's in-flight focus_paths[] and refuses bare 'git add .' / '-A' plus any path outside that scope — prevents multi-agent commit-interleave in a shared checkout. --no-coordination escape hatch for single-agent work.

skills.summary: One-line plain-English reminder of what you were just working on. Drops into chat for ADHD-shape memory relief.
skills.use_when: Operator types /im-fried when they've lost the thread and want a single sentence to re-orient. No flags, no follow-up — one line, then done.

skills.summary: Read-only synthesizer that walks the kaizen.md substrate across all SKILL.md files, clusters observations, and emits a PROPOSED-EDITS report. Hermes pattern — never mutates source artifacts.
skills.use_when: After kaizen substrate has accumulated for >7 days, to surface cross-skill drift patterns and propose corpus-wide ergonomics/correctness fixes. Trigger phrases: 'where is the kaizen drift', 'what's the cross-skill pattern', 'synthesize kaizen', 'propose skill improvements', 'improve skills'. Output is a markdown proposed-edits report; operator decides which proposals to action via /create-workorder.

skills.summary: Live registry of which agent is currently working in which scope. Sidecar at LOGS/SESSIONS/{date}/in-flight.json. Subcommands: --register, --heartbeat, --clear, --list, --sweep. Atomic writes. Read by /im-fried, task-board, /stage-mine.
skills.use_when: An agent needs to (a) declare its current focus + allowed file scope, (b) periodically heartbeat to stay live, (c) clear on completion, (d) query who else is in-flight, or (e) sweep stale entries via cron/bootstrap.

skills.summary: Spawn a Chrome MCP tab for an agent identity on the /sessions chat surface. Returns tabId for subsequent /send-chat --via-browser calls. Page auto-initializes identity from ?as=DOMAIN. Use ONCE per agent per session.
skills.use_when: Use once per agent per session to join the /sessions chat surface as that agent, or when the user invokes /join-chat-as-agent.

skills.summary: Agent domains check in to a daily session and write checkin.json/status.json (concurrent, lockless), then run /check-dispatch-inbox inline and arm the wake-poll loop. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: An agent domain needs to check in to a daily orchestration session

skills.summary: Reader/projection rebuilder for the continuous-improvement loop. Queries kaizen_entries DB; regenerates kaizen.md projections; surfaces missing-goal + dead-skill + top-category clusters.
skills.use_when: Operator wants to see what agents have been observing across the corpus. Run after a long session OR on-demand for review OR after bulk edits to regenerate sidecars from canonical DB.

skills.summary: KAIZEN-family improvement-capture (renamed from /improvements). Logs findings to the canonical registry.db improvement_entries table; per-scope improvements.json files are projections regenerated from the DB (Option 2c, mirrors kaizen-log + kaizen.md sidecars).
skills.use_when: Use when the user asks to review code, audit a scope, capture an improvement idea, or invokes /kaizen-improvements (formerly /improvements).

skills.summary: Writer half of the kaizen loop: appends a categorized observation to a skill's kaizen.md sidecar + registry.db, worktree-aware so the append commits with the work; bug-shaped notes surface in real time. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: A skill agent finishes a noteworthy run and wants to log friction (performance / ergonomics / correctness / observability). Called from the canonical 'Kaizen Step' block in every SKILL.md per the universal contract.

skills.summary: Env-gated validator for the kaizen contract. Checks kaizen.md sidecar presence + non-template ## Goal + SKILL.md universal-contract Kaizen Step block (post-P5). CODEREF_KAIZEN_GATE=1 to refuse.
skills.use_when: Inside /new-coderef-skill --update or --create to enforce kaizen contract; standalone to validate a skill's kaizen compliance.

skills.summary: Canonical entity keyer: stamps a deterministic ps_uid onto person/place/org/artifact rows per the §3 disambiguator ladder, including the rung-3 RECORD-RELATIONSHIP anchor for witnesses and investigative-body members. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Existing entity rows (live DB place/organization/person/artifact tables, the gazetteer config/*.json, or ENTITY-SEEDS CSVs) need a deterministic ps_uid stamped on. Invoke to compute ps_uid via psm/ps_uid.py per the §3 per-type disambiguator ladder, enforce the R5 refuse-to-mint guard + R6 resolved-only + R7/name-first dedup + the QID label-matches-name agreement gate + neutral/factual naming, run the live-schema preflight and begin/rollback rehearsal, then commit. Driven end-to-end by /onboard-entity.

skills.summary: Cross-domain mailbox overview: enumerates domains with mailboxes and renders inbox/sent/drafts counts per domain. Pairs with /send-email + /read-mailbox. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Operator or agent wants a quick view of who has mail. Useful at session start or before invoking /read-mailbox to see which domains have new traffic.

skills.summary: Lock the active agent onto ONE specific task. Refuses all other workstreams until /lock-in --unlock. Per-domain lock state at LOGS/SESSIONS/{session}/{DOMAIN}/lock-in.json. --whats-next introspects pending locked work.
skills.use_when: Operator wants laser focus on a single thread of work. Engages with task description; releases with --unlock; introspects with --whats-next.

skills.summary: Research a business idea, analyze viability, and generate a standardized business idea package.
skills.use_when: Use when the user invokes /log-business-idea or asks to log, research, analyze, and report on a business idea in a controlled standard format.

skills.summary: Generates a YAML session log capturing tools, files, docs, decisions, and outcomes, with mandatory closure_verification for doc-discovery, heartbeats, and cleanup. History in SKILL.md metadata.changelog.
skills.use_when: Generates a YAML session log capturing tools used, files modified, documents created, decisions, and outcomes. Use when user says "log session", "save session", "create session log", "/log-session", or "write a session summary". Saves to .coderef/sessions/ by default. Never includes conversation transcript.

skills.summary: Append one compact skill-activity entry to the active daily session domain activity log.
skills.use_when: Use when the user asks to log skills, record skill usage, write an activity-log entry, or close a workorder with a concise list of invoked skills and outcomes.

skills.summary: Archive, scored-triage, and rebuild an agent's auto-memory so MEMORY.md loads in full and carries only durable facts; --prune is lighter periodic hygiene. Always writes the domain-from-cwd invariant. Use when MEMORY.md is over-limit, stale, or self-conflicting.
skills.use_when: MEMORY.md exceeds the load limit, is partly stale/superseded, self-conflicting, or has accreted closed-ticket work-logs that duplicate git history. Also use --prune for periodic memory hygiene.

skills.summary: Briefly log an app or dev idea as a single scannable markdown file under CODEREF/APPS/{slug}/idea.md. Capture-only — no web research.
skills.use_when: User wants to quickly stash an app or dev idea without the deep 4-file research package log-business-idea produces. No tracking registry writes.

skills.summary: Create, update, archive, or supersede an ecosystem skill with paired SKILL.md + skill.json, thin wrappers (claude/codex/agy), and auto-rebuild of tracking/skills.json. Enforces signpost-shape on summary/description. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: User asks to create a new ecosystem skill, rename a local skill, update an existing skill, install a wrapper, archive a superseded skill, or align skill surfaces to the local CodeRef standard. Runs rebuild_skills_tracking.py at the end of every pass so tracking/skills.json is always derived from the manifests currently on disk.

skills.summary: Wake-poll nudge for a stalled agent — fires a minimal instruction-pass dispatch when an agent's wake-poll appears dead.
skills.use_when: An agent has pending dispatches in their inbox AND their status.json hasn't updated in >10 min. Refuses to fire if inbox is empty (no noise).

skills.summary: Onboard a JFK-corpus collection by deriving a facts-only, ps_uid-keyed sidecar artifact with a closed schema and structured provenance.
skills.use_when: A held or discoverable collection lacks the structured metadata the census needs (no TOC, no online-status map, no keyed index) and those facts must be derived onto the existing ps_uid spine WITHOUT copying source artifacts.

skills.summary: Workflow verb that drives /key-entity end to end: read entity manifest/seed -> validate (refuse-to-mint) -> live-schema preflight -> begin/rollback rehearse -> commit -> report all-surfaces. Entity twin of /onboard-keying.
skills.use_when: Existing entity rows (live DB place/organization/person/artifact, gazetteer config/*.json, ENTITY-SEEDS CSVs) need a deterministic ps_uid stamped on and verified consistent across every surface. Invoke to read the seed, validate against the R5 refuse-to-mint gate + R6 resolved-only + neutral naming, run the §4.1 live-schema preflight, rehearse begin/rollback on LIVE, hold for the combined operator go, commit, stamp all surfaces, and report keyed/distinct/collisions. Surfaces status:blocked rather than guessing an ambiguous identity or committing without the go.

skills.summary: Package a canonical CodeRef skill or generic workflow as a ChatGPT Custom GPT bundle (instructions txt, upload list, starters, capabilities).
skills.use_when: User asks to create a GPT, clone a skill into a GPT, onboard a ChatGPT agent, or invokes /onboard-gpt-agent.

skills.summary: Step 1 of the Collection Keying Standard: inspect a read-only native index and write a validated keying.json, classifying the collection as flat/hierarchical/partial with deferred_grains. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: A JFK-corpus collection has a native index (master.csv + upstream exports) but no keying.json yet, so the keyer cannot run. Invoke to decide source_token/native_id_column/normalization/grain and write a keying.json that passes validate_keying.py. Surface status:blocked for ambiguous columns or unpinned (private-archive) tokens instead of guessing.

skills.summary: Mark a workorder as PENDING for autonomous execution by WorkorderWatcher. Idempotent write of status=PENDING + picked_at + picker to the workorder communication.json.
skills.use_when: Use to hand a workorder to autonomous execution (WorkorderWatcher), or when the user invokes /pick.

skills.summary: Author the next phase's tasks for a rolling-plan workorder from fresh phase-scoped discovery, then promote the linked stub deferred_phase->promoted via registry-db; worktree-aware on the WO plan.json. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: After a rolling-plan workorder's prior phase completes and is reviewed, the next plan_deferred phase needs its task list authored from fresh discovery before /execute-workorder resumes. Companion to /create-workorder --phased-stubs.

skills.summary: Read a workorder's scope plus acceptance criteria and emit a recommended test matrix as JSON. Helps plan authors avoid ad-hoc test discipline.
skills.use_when: Authoring plan.json for a new workorder. Invoke during /create-workorder Step 6 (or manually before plan.json is finalized) to map each acceptance criterion to a test category, suggested file path, fixture pattern, and invariant flag.

skills.summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE populate-coderef CLI to generate/refresh .coderef/ artifacts for a target project.
skills.use_when: User asks to scan, populate, regenerate, or refresh .coderef/ for a project. Preferred scan-producing surface; supersedes the older element-only coderef-scan bin.

skills.summary: Generate print-ready plain-text process reports from workflows, notes, code findings, or examples.
skills.use_when: Use when the user wants a printable report, process guide, implementation guide, SOP, developer guide, or report based on supplied notes, workflows, files, or examples.

skills.summary: Author a grounded, multi-pillar executive overview report for any project or domain.
skills.use_when: User asks for an executive/overview report, a 'what is this and why does it matter' document, an onboarding overview, or a capability map for any project, codebase, business operation, or system.
reads: {project} project-context / docs / source / org or process maps (for grounding)

skills.summary: Mark a workorder PENDING for autonomous WorkorderWatcher dequeue. Idempotent write of status=PENDING + picked_at + picker to communication.json. Supersedes /pick.
skills.use_when: Workorder plan is approved and ready for autonomous ROUTER execution. Renamed from /pick (WO-CREATE-WORKORDER-FROM-STUB-AND-RENAME-PICK-001).

skills.summary: Promote TxEN recipes into @entity/design with DESIGN standards enforcement and testing matrix validation
skills.use_when: User wants to graduate a recipe from runtime-catalog or app-local TSX into the @entity/design public API so multiple tenants can import it. Sibling to /create-txen-recipe — second half of the two-step pipeline.

skills.summary: Route a stub to its category-appropriate next-step command per category-rules.json in one move.
skills.use_when: Operator or agent wants to advance a stub past the /create-workorder Step 2a rejection_message friction; routes WO categories to /create-workorder and non-WO categories (idea/research/skill-creation) to their canonical authoring tools.

skills.summary: Promote an artifact into the operator's AT-BAT slot on the daily-session task board. Same artifact-shape regex as /lock-in; refuses freeform. Writes task-board-promotions.json AND calls /lock-in (at-bat IS the operator's lock-in).
skills.use_when: Operator wants to set the active work for the day. Combines task-board ordering with the canonical /lock-in primitive so a single command both surfaces the work in the AT-BAT slot AND engages refuse-other-work discipline.

skills.summary: Promote an artifact (skill / WO / STUB / DISPATCH) into the operator's IN-THE-HOLE slot (third, after at-bat and on-deck) on the daily-session task board. Same artifact-shape regex as /lock-in; persists to LOGS/SESSIONS/{today}/task-board-promotions.json.
skills.use_when: Operator wants to mark which artifact is queued after the current at-bat and on-deck work. Sets task-board ordering deterministically for the third slot rather than relying on the recency-default.

skills.summary: Promote an artifact (skill / WO / STUB / DISPATCH) into the operator's ON-DECK slot on the daily-session task board. Same artifact-shape regex as /lock-in; refuses freeform. Writes to LOGS/SESSIONS/{today}/task-board-promotions.json so the slot persists across re-renders.
skills.use_when: Operator wants to mark which artifact is queued for after the current at-bat work. Sets task-board ordering deterministically rather than relying on the recency-default.

skills.summary: Storage/schema authority for Primary Sources DB — migrations, catalog, generated types, live verification. Sibling to ps-scanner and ps-seeder.
skills.use_when: Use when an agent needs to inspect, design, migrate, verify, document, or safely operate the Primary Sources Supabase database.
skills.resource: supabase:primary-sources (external Postgres DB; project identified in C:\Users\willh\Desktop\CODEREF\PROJECTS\primary-sources\supabase\config.toml)

skills.summary: Scanner authority for Primary Sources — pipeline, OCR modes, 19-cluster/45-knob engine_tuning, artifacts, Supabase linker, reviewer UUID roundtrip. Sibling to ps-db-manager and ps-seeder.
skills.use_when: Use when an agent needs to inspect, change, debug, document, or validate the Primary Sources scanner, OCR pipeline, scan artifacts, visual verifier, classifier handoff, engine_tuning knobs, or entity extraction handoff. Pair with ps-seeder when changes affect operator-facing feedback or knob suggestions.

skills.summary: Run-and-report + agent-driven reviewer for Primary Sources. Surfaces feedback, suggests Tier-0 knob changes (19/45 surface), self-reviews via Class A-F rubric. Sibling to ps-db-manager and ps-scanner.
skills.use_when: User wants to run a scan and get actionable feedback (gazetteer + knob recommendations), or wants to analyze an existing scan's entities artifact, or wants to propose new gazetteer entries from unmatched canonical candidates, or wants an agent-driven quality review of mention output via --reviewer-mode. Distinct from ps-scanner (architectural authority).

skills.summary: Analyze CodeRef quality.json and diagrams/ artifacts; extract normalized metrics and trend deltas across runs.
skills.use_when: Reviewing a workorder for code-quality regressions before close, building a quality dashboard, or running pre-merge gate checks. Read-only analysis over /coderef-pipeline output.

skills.summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE rag-index CLI to build semantic search index with vector embeddings.
skills.use_when: User wants to build or rebuild the RAG index for a project. Pair with /rag-search for querying.

skills.summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE rag-search CLI for natural-language semantic code search.
skills.use_when: User asks a natural-language question about code in a project that already has a RAG index built via /rag-index.

skills.summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE rag-status CLI to inspect RAG index metadata (read-only).
skills.use_when: User wants to check whether a RAG index exists for a project, or inspect its provider/store/language coverage.

skills.summary: Read a domain's mailbox folder (inbox/sent/drafts) and render messages as a table; --json mode for machine consumption, --thread for conversation filtering. Pairs with /send-email + /list-mailboxes. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Agent or operator wants to scan a domain's mailbox -- list pending messages, follow a thread, or inspect sent items. Pairs with /send-email + /list-mailboxes.

skills.summary: Register a new agent domain atomically across agent-domains.json + seed-domains.md with resolver-glob validation; --update edits an existing row's non-identity fields (hosts_under/glob/etc) via registry_db. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Operator needs to add a new agent domain to the CodeRef ecosystem. Replaces the manual 3-file edit dance that caused the 2026-05-24 SOURCE-MATERIAL ghost-row incident (registry pointed at non-existent path). Use this skill any time a new domain is being created, including when extracting a sub-domain from an existing parent via hosts_under nesting.

skills.summary: Register the current project in CodeRef TRACKING via the writer-adapter. Prefers .coderef and foundation-docs as evidence; falls back to repo inspection.
skills.use_when: Use when an agent is inside a project and needs to register that project with the central CodeRef tracking system.

skills.summary: TRACKING registry.db library wrapping the SQLite-backed canonical registry. Insert with collision detection, query by legacy_id or ULID, hot-cold flip, retention sweep. Used by migration scripts and writer skills.
skills.use_when: Use when reading/writing the canonical TRACKING/registry.db (insert, update_and_export, commit_and_export, query by legacy_id/ULID) or invoking /registry-db.

skills.summary: Automated hygiene tool for the CodeRef tracking registries. Scans terminal-state items, proposes mutations for user approval, enforces schema compliance.
skills.use_when: User asks to clean up TRACKING/, sweep stale workorders, find registry orphans/ghosts, or runs periodic maintenance. Also invoked via OpenClaw coderef-dispatch 'tracking sweep' grammar.

skills.summary: Audit and review a workorder for quality and compliance, including a git-doctor stranded-branch subcheck that flags unmerged WO branches before close. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: User says "review workorder", "audit workorder", "/review-workorder", or when validating a workorder before archiving.

skills.summary: Analyze validate-routes output: coverage %, ranked route collisions, per-method breakdown.
skills.use_when: Pre-merge check on routing changes, API surface audits, or building a routing health dashboard. Read-only analysis over /validate-routes output.

skills.summary: Activity-rollup companion to /session-status. Aggregates unified-events.jsonl across all checked-in domains into per-domain ran/shipped/stuck/errored rollup over a time window.
skills.use_when: Operator (or another agent) asks 'what happened in the last N minutes' / 'anything stuck?' / 'what shipped recently?'. Single-shot, read-only, deterministic. Sibling of /session-status (state-snapshot).

skills.summary: Stash a scannable domain-specific report into the current domain's USER/ directory. User supplies topic and length; agent fills a 5-section template.
skills.use_when: User wants a fast on-disk summary of a domain's tools/process/whatever they can re-read or hand off. Outside the tracked-work surface — no workorder, dispatch, or registry entry created.

skills.summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE scan-frontend-calls CLI to detect fetch/axios/request patterns.
skills.use_when: User wants an inventory of frontend API call sites, or as a prerequisite for /validate-routes.

skills.summary: Wrap ScheduleWakeup with arg validation, [60,3600] clamp, presets, --cancel sentinel. Step 4.5 passive-andon-pull gate fires a blocker dispatch to ASSISTANT BEFORE wake when the agent is waiting on user/agent/assignment.
skills.use_when: Re-arm wake-poll loop at the end of a session/workflow skill, cancel polling via --cancel, OR auto-pull andon passive (renamed from waiting-ping) when waiting (v1.1.0+).

skills.summary: Schedule a cron job from an audit, procedure, or workflow file. Generates workorder stub+plan+communication and registers in Automation engine schedules.json.
skills.use_when: User wants to schedule a recurring audit, scan, watchdog, or workflow to run on a cron schedule. Discovers procedure files in a project, gathers requirements via conversation, and registers the job.

skills.summary: Search the keyed JFK corpus (WC/DPD/NARA) — probe coverage, retrieve via the keyed-entity join + full-text, return sectioned ps_uid-anchored cited snippets. Use when an operator wants page-level quotable evidence about a person/topic/event already on the spine.
skills.use_when: An operator asks 'what records exist about X' or 'find evidence that X' over the already-keyed JFK corpus and wants page-level cited snippets. NOT for gathering new sources (use /gather-jfk-sources) or onboarding/keying collections.

skills.summary: Send a chat message from an agent into the /sessions chat surface. Direct POST to write-helper (default) OR via Chrome MCP composer (--via-browser). Tone-checked at daemon boundary; refused with 422 if dispatch-speak detected.
skills.use_when: Use when an agent wants to communicate via the bidirectional chat instead of structured dispatches, or the user invokes /send-chat.

skills.summary: Send an internal agent-to-agent email: writes the envelope to the sender's sent/ + each recipient's inbox/, with cc + threading via --in-reply-to. Internal-only; --external rejected pending Phase 2. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Agent wants to send a conversation-shaped message to one or more other agents. Different mental model than dispatch.json (command-shaped). Supports threading via --in-reply-to.

skills.summary: Scaffold a new backend processing service into Entity with tenant manifests, worker/engine directories, and Next.js API proxy
skills.use_when: Scaffold a new backend processing service into Entity with tenant manifests, worker/engine directories, and Next.js API proxy

skills.summary: Canonical session aggregator. Reads active-work + per-domain status/checkin/dispatch + recent git log; emits ONE consistent status block.
skills.use_when: Operator asks 'status' / 'what's up' / 'what's everyone doing' / 'who's idle'. Single-shot, read-only, deterministic. Replaces ad-hoc bash for-loops with a contracted output shape.

skills.summary: Analyze existing sites or compose new ones against composition standards. Generates scannable markdown maps showing shells, sections, recipes, templates, components, data flows, state schemas, and validation rules.
skills.use_when: User wants to analyze site composition, generate composition specs, or validate site architecture against composition standards.

skills.summary: Bare wrapper around Lloyd's spawn: resolve --<modelname> to provider/endpoint, call Lloyd, return PID+log or text. Terminal flavors support --continue (worktree-aware) + conventional tab colors per domain group (full table in references/DOMAIN-TAB-COLORS.md).
skills.use_when: Operator wants to spawn a single agent against any model (local Ollama or frontier CLI) without learning the underlying endpoint surface. Use --qwen / --phi4-mini / --auto for local; --gemini / --claude-opus / --claude-sonnet / --codex for frontier background spawns; --claude-cli / --agy-cli / --codex-cli for interactive Windows-Terminal windows (--gemini-cli is deprecated -> --agy-cli; add --continue to resume a crashed session via `claude --continue` or `agy --continue`, --tab-color to color the tab). Frontier spawns require --workorder (auto-created if absent).

skills.summary: Spawn a headless agent to refresh a domain's CodeRef index and foundation documentation with semantic header generation (@layer, @capability, @constraint tags enabled by default).
skills.use_when: Post-close-out hygiene, pre-review-workorder, scheduled maintenance, or after semantic annotation enablement.

skills.summary: Scoped git-add wrapper. Reads in-flight focus_paths[] and refuses bare `git add .` / `-A` + paths outside scope. --no-coordination escape hatch for single-agent. Prevents multi-agent commit-interleave.
skills.use_when: Before staging files for a commit in a multi-agent session. Replaces direct `git add` calls when in-flight focus_paths is set. Bypassable for single-agent sessions via --no-coordination.

skills.summary: Compose START-HERE.md + RESUME-PROMPT.md living session-state snapshot at ASSISTANT root; scans 6 sources; optional HTML render with graceful fallback.
skills.use_when: Invoke before stepping away from a session, at session-end ceremony via /close-daily-session, or after major WO-close commits (gate-flagged). Produces a 9-section drop-in resume doc for any agent or the operator picking the session up cold.

skills.summary: Restart all session tracking services on demand - webhook monitor, baseline snapshots, attendance tracking
skills.use_when: ORCHESTRATOR or any agent needs to restart session tracking services (webhook, baseline, attendance)

skills.summary: Capture quick ideas as stubs; writes directly to registry.db via commit_and_export with zero dispatches, plus a --goal non-interactive mode for zero-prompt mint by /auto-workorder --auto-stub. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Capture a quick idea as a stub in the centralized backlog. Creates stub.json in coderef/working/{PROJECT}/{feature-name}/ and registers it in TRACKING/stubs.json via direct registry-db write (zero dispatches) with auto-incremented STUB-ID. Use when user says "stub", "new idea", "capture idea", "log idea", or "/stub".

skills.summary: High-level architectural lead for SURFACES-HTML. Owns page composition, site-wide nav inventory, cross-component coordination seams. Delegates component authoring to /surfaces-html-designer.
skills.use_when: User asks for page-level or suite-level SURFACES-HTML work: add a new page, link a page in the sidebar, compose a dashboard from existing components, decide where a new shared concern lives (CSS var seam vs event bus vs state-layer addition), maintain the site-wide nav inventory. Sibling to /surfaces-html-designer (component level) and /design-component (React stack).

skills.summary: Standards-aware authoring helper for SURFACES-HTML components. Knows tokens, BEM, one-way dependency, event bus, doc-code mirror.
skills.use_when: User asks to add a component to the SURFACES-HTML suite (a card, table, badge, drawer, panel, etc. living under ASSISTANT/SURFACES/surfaces-html/src/components/). Sibling to /design-component which targets React/TSX/Tailwind for Entity-style apps; this skill targets native HTML/CSS/JS only.

skills.summary: Compose HTML renders from agent artifacts via SURFACES-HTML components and emit a URL; --markdown renders a .md file to a self-contained, file://-openable, mobile-responsive page. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Phase 2 of STUB-434 agent-output HTML pipeline. Use when a calling skill (any of the 88 inventoried) wants to render its artifact as a rich HTML page. Pass --skill={name} for auto-template resolution; the rule-tree decides whether render is possible and which template applies.

skills.summary: Render the daily-session aggregate as a living HTML dashboard. Aggregates per-domain checkin/status/dispatch/activity-log/mapping-table artifacts into a single bento-grid surface. Session-keyed URL; subsequent invocations re-snapshot state.
skills.use_when: Operator wants a single dashboard URL surfacing all daily-session state across all agent domains — checkins, status, dispatches, activity logs, mapping-tables — that updates live as the session continues.

skills.summary: Sync TRACKING/llm-models.json with Ollama /api/tags. Upsert keyed by sha256 digest; refresh API-owned fields; preserve human-owned fields (role, context_window, capabilities, notes). Tag aliasing merged into tags[].
skills.use_when: Use when syncing the local LLM model registry against Ollama, or the user invokes /sync-llm-models.

skills.summary: Orchestrate tenant onboarding with DESIGN standards token system and branding compliance
skills.use_when: Orchestrate agentic tenant onboarding using the completed intake form and the active apps/web onboarding playbook

skills.summary: File a bug as a structured TKT-XXXXXX ticket: writes registry.db (tickets), auto-mints a linked category=fix stub that feeds /create-workorder --from-stub, dedups at entry. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Use when you hit an error or identify a debug target and want it tracked as a real ticket (not a throwaway note). Triggers: "/ticket", "file a bug", "open a ticket", "log this bug". DEBUG-owned.

skills.summary: Schema-aware query tool for the CodeRef tracking directory + improvement logs. Default returns an attention-first roll-up; rich token grammar filters by entity type, scope, id, status, severity, stale/recent/all. v3.x loads schemas at invocation.
skills.use_when: Use when the user asks for status, tracking, progress, what needs attention, or any read-only query about workorders/sessions/stubs/projects/skills/improvements, or invokes /tracking.

skills.summary: Validate tracking/ registry files via a schema pass, a dereference pass, and a terminal-state diff pass; --scope narrows it to one project's rows for a scoped close. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: The user asks to validate tracking registries (workorders.json, sessions.json, etc.) or when a tracking integrity check is needed after batch updates.

skills.summary: Create valid TxEN page-drafts with DESIGN standards token system compliance and Phase 1 header alignment rules
skills.use_when: Create or revise one valid TxEN page-draft JSON file for Entity's current TxEN maker-workspace workflow with DESIGN standards compliance

skills.summary: Orchestrate Discord webhook status updates with 5-phase execution, data gathering, formatting, and delivery verification.
skills.use_when: A unified visibility update needs to be pushed to Discord combining agent activity, workorder status, stubs, improvements, and attendance metrics.

skills.summary: Refresh master ecosystem documentation index from TRACKING/ registries
skills.use_when: TRACKING/ state changes significantly, or before closing a workorder that affects registries

skills.summary: In-flight maintenance of an existing roadmap (atomic .md + .json rewrite); domain roadmaps get --link-stub/--link-wo/--goal-status/--rerender, auto-detected. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Operator needs to flip phase status, record ship commit, add items/phases/callouts, reconcile markdown edits, or maintain a domain roadmap (link a promoted stub/WO to a goal, flip goal status, re-render md from canonical json).

skills.summary: Self-report current work state in the active daily session by updating status.json with state, task, and timestamp.
skills.use_when: Your work state changes: starting a new task, finishing work, getting blocked, or transitioning between active and idle.

skills.summary: Register or update an item in the centralized TRACKING/ directory. Called by creation workflows (/create-workorder, /create-autoresearch-loop, /stub) to ensure every new item has...
skills.use_when: Register or update an item in the centralized TRACKING/ directory. Called by creation workflows (/create-workorder, /create-autoresearch-loop, /stub) to ensure every new item has central visibility. Also callable directly for manual updates.

skills.summary: Validator companion to align-coderef-culture. Audits session artifacts against 13 locked checks and emits PASS/WARNING/FAIL verdict. Used by /close-workorder PHASE A AUDIT.
skills.use_when: Before close-workorder PHASE A AUDIT (Step 3), or standalone to get an early cultural-compliance read.

skills.summary: Pre-flight ecosystem health check. Verifies Lloyd API connectivity (port 52847) and CodeRef Core index drift (.coderef/reports/drift.json). Run at workorder execution start to catch infra-level desync before work begins.
skills.use_when: Use at workorder execution start to catch infra-level desync, or when the user invokes /validate-ecosystem-sync.

skills.summary: Validate field-shape parity across CSV/JSON/DB for person/place/organization entity surfaces in primary-sources
skills.use_when: Before authoring CSV→DB loaders or DB→JSON exporters, after applying a migration touching person/place/organization tables, after hand-editing JSON gazetteers, after changing any jfk-key-*.csv header, or as a CI smoke test before a scanner deploy that depends on gazetteer shape

skills.summary: Invoke CODEREF-CORE validate-routes CLI to cross-check frontend API calls vs backend routes.
skills.use_when: User wants to validate frontend/backend route alignment, detect 404/405 risks, or find unused server routes.

skills.summary: Runtime validator for the SKILL-CONTRACTS.md trigger-phrase taxonomy: scans SKILL.md for enforceable-contract phrasing and requires runtime-gate evidence OR an agent_honored exemption; --report=dashboard breaks down exempt reasons. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: After any skill mutation, or as a periodic audit. Phase 3 of WO-SKILL-ENFORCEMENT-CONSOLIDATION-001 wires this into /new-coderef-skill --update as a hard gate.

skills.summary: Canonical post-ship summary: reads a git commit (default HEAD) and emits Markdown to stdout + a JSON payload to stderr, detecting per-package version bumps via skill.json diff. Auto-invoked at /close-workorder. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: After a commit lands, agent or operator wants a standardized post-ship summary block (replaces ad-hoc prose). Auto-fires at /close-workorder; standalone-callable any time.
reads: git history (via git show / git diff)

skills.summary: Find recently modified Markdown or project files across active tracked projects with a packaged HTML report script.
skills.use_when: Use when the user asks /wheres-it-at, wants to find recent Markdown files quickly, or needs a local HTML report of recently modified files across active projects.

skills.summary: Grep-rank lookup helper over SKILLS/STANDARDS/SKILL-INDEX.md. Pass a topic; returns top-N ranked canonical skill names with category, slash command, one-line summary. Use BEFORE freehanding a skill name.
skills.use_when: Operator or agent needs a canonical skill name for a topic but doesn't want to guess. Companion to /session-status (state-snapshot) and /runtime-state (activity-rollup) at the discoverability layer.

skills.summary: Close-time follow-up capture for /close-workorder Step 16: scrapes 7 sources (5 in-WO fields + unified-events observations + git commit bodies) for follow-up mentions and writes confirmed entries as candidate stubs for operator triage. History in references/CHANGELOG.md.
skills.use_when: Invoked from /close-workorder Step 16 ritual after WO ship + before archive ceremony. Standalone invocation for re-capture on closed WOs. Goal: prevent tribal-knowledge follow-ups from evaporating at WO close.
